name: Rein Therapeutics, Inc.
business: Rein Therapeutics is a clinical stage biopharmaceutical company focused on developing novel therapies for the treatment of orphan pulmonary and fibrosis indications. Its lead product candidate, LTI-03, is a peptide for Idiopathic Pulmonary Fibrosis (IPF) currently in a Phase 2 clinical trial. Development of its other candidate, LTI-01 for loculated pleural effusion, has been paused indefinitely due to capital limitations.
sellsTo: Patients with loculated pleural effusion (LPE) (development paused)

exposures.sentence: We are a clinical stage biopharmaceutical company focused on developing novel therapies for the treatment of orphan pulmonary and fibrosis indications with no approved or limited effective treatments.

catalysts.event: Received EMA authorization to initiate Phase 2 RENEW trial of LTI-03 in Germany and Poland
catalysts.sentence: In October 2025, we received authorization from the European Medicines Agency, or the EMA, to initiate our Phase 2 RENEW trial of our lead candidate, LTI-03, for the treatment of IPF at sites in Germany and Poland.

catalysts.event: Received orphan drug designation from EMA for LTI-03
catalysts.sentence: In January 2026, we received orphan drug designation from the EMA for LTI-03.

catalysts.event: First patient dosed in Phase 2 RENEW trial of LTI-03
catalysts.sentence: In March 2026, we dosed our first patient in the RENEW Phase 2 clinical trial of LTI-03.

catalysts.event: Expected initial interim topline data from Phase 2 RENEW trial
catalysts.sentence: We expect to report initial interim topline data on some proportion of patients in the fourth quarter of 2026.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 100,000 people living with IPF in the U.S.
tamClaims.sentence: There are approximately 100,000 people living with IPF in the U.S.

tamClaims.claim: Over 60,000 cases of LPE associated with CPE and empyema occur annually in the U.S.
tamClaims.sentence: We believe over 60,000 cases of LPE associated with CPE and empyema are estimated to occur annually in the U.S. alone, and based upon our market research, over half of these patients are receiving off-label, intrapleural fibrinolytic therapy, or IPFT, which is the use of clot busting drugs injected locally into the pleural cavity to treat the LPEs.
